entities.name: śabda
entities.cite: The sabda meant here is ajasra-vrtti, of unceasing operation

entities.name: śabda (subtle form)
entities.cite: the inner word-nature of 1.112, never silent, only suksma, too subtle for apprehension

entities.name: vyajana
entities.cite: the fan does not create it but pracyate, concentrates and quickens it past the threshold of feeling

entities.name: abhivyakti
entities.cite: abhivyakti and not production was the right category from 1.44 onward

axioms.name: ajasra_vrtti
axioms.cite: The sabda meant here is ajasra-vrtti, of unceasing operation

axioms.name: anupalabdha
axioms.cite: only suksma, too subtle for apprehension
the inner word-nature of 1.112, never silent, only suksma, too subtle for apprehension

axioms.name: sva_nimitta
axioms.cite: articulation is the fan, a sva-nimitta that raises the perpetual subtle operation to gross perceptibility for a moment

axioms.name: abhivyakti
axioms.cite: articulation is the fan, a sva-nimitta that raises the perpetual subtle operation to gross perceptibility for a moment

axioms.name: satya_anupalabdhi_anutthita
axioms.cite: Its non-appearance is thus an epistemic fact, not an ontological absence

denials.name: utpatti
denials.cite: one does not produce what never pauses; one occasions its detection

claims.name: ajasra_vrtti
claims.cite: The word whose functioning is unceasing

claims.name: anupalabdha
claims.cite: is not apprehended because of its subtlety

claims.name: abhivyakti
claims.cite: it is made manifest through its own occasioning cause

rejected_readings.label: productive_utterance
rejected_readings.rendering: The utterance produces the word from nothing.
rejected_readings.why: Asserts production (utpatti) of sabda by vyajana. The commentary explicitly denies this: 'one does not produce what never pauses; one occasions its detection.' The correct category is manifestation (abhivyakti), not creation.

rejected_readings.label: episodic_word_nature
rejected_readings.rendering: The word ceases to exist when not articulated, only coming into being through utterance.
rejected_readings.why: Treats unmanifested sabda as ontologically non-existent (asat) rather than epistemically imperceptible. The commentary explicitly rules this out: 'Its non-appearance is thus an epistemic fact, not an ontological absence.' Sabda eternally operates; non-apprehension reflects limitation of perception, not lack of being.

rejected_readings.label: no_perpetual_subtle_operation
rejected_readings.rendering: The word pauses or ceases entirely when not being articulated.
rejected_readings.why: Denies the axiom of ajasra-vrtti (unceasing functioning). The verse and commentary insist: 'sabda meant here is ajasra-vrtti, of unceasing operation' and 'one does not produce what never pauses.' This contradicts the foundational claim of eternal subtle operation.

notes: Encodes the epistemology of subtle manifestation: sabda eternally operates in unceasing subtle form (ajasra-vrtti, suksma) but remains imperceptible due to its subtlety. Utterance (vyajana) occasions manifestation (abhivyakti)—not through production but by concentrating/quickening the perpetual subtle operation past the threshold of gross perceptibility. The contrast between ontological presence and epistemic absence (non-apprehension) is central.
